commit.message:
fix: remove blocking base node call in TransactionServiceRequest::FetchUnspentOutputs (#7724)

The `FetchUnspentOutputs` handler was awaiting
`fetch_unspent_outputs_from_node` directly in the service loop, blocking
all other request processing if base node connectivity stalled.

## Changes

- **Non-blocking dispatch**: `FetchUnspentOutputs` in `handle_request`
now uses `reply_channel.take()` + `return Ok(())` instead of blocking
with `async { ... }.await`, consistent with the
`GetFeePerGramStatsPerBlock` pattern.
- **New `handle_fetch_unspent_outputs_request`**: Clones the
connectivity handle, then `tokio::spawn`s the base node RPC work,
sending the result back through the reply channel when complete.
- **Removed `fetch_unspent_outputs_from_node`**: The old `&mut self`
async method is no longer needed.

```rust
// Before — blocks the service loop on base node I/O
TransactionServiceRequest::FetchUnspentOutputs { output_hashes } => {
    async {
        let unspent_outputs = self.fetch_unspent_outputs_from_node(output_hashes).await?;
        Ok(TransactionServiceResponse::UnspentOutputs(unspent_outputs))
    }
    .await
},

// After — dispatches to a background task, returns immediately
TransactionServiceRequest::FetchUnspentOutputs { output_hashes } => {
    let reply_channel = reply_channel.take().expect("reply_channel is Some");
    self.handle_fetch_unspent_outputs_request(output_hashes, reply_channel);
    return Ok(());
},
```

commit.message:
Add Base L2 blockchain stability analyzer (base_data_gen.py)

Scores Base L2 on-chain data using FSR Predictability Score:
- BLOCK_TIME: inter-block finality intervals (target ~2s on Base)
- GAS: base fee per gas in Gwei — market/infrastructure stability
- TX_COUNT: transactions per block — network throughput consistency

Uses Base's public JSON-RPC (no API key required).
K-factor set to 2.0 (finance-grade sensitivity for infrastructure).
Includes live stream mode for continuous re-fetch + Twitch overlay integration.
Dark-themed charts saved to static/images/base_charts/.

Add full-stack smart parking system with:

Backend (FastAPI):
- Async SQLAlchemy 2.0 models: User, ParkingLot, ParkingSpace, OccupancyLog
- Routes: auth (register/login/me), parking (lots/spaces/status), admin
- Services: auth (JWT/bcrypt), parking, detection integration
- Pydantic v2 schemas with validation
- Alembic async migration setup
- Role-based access control (driver/staff/admin)

Detection module:
- ParkingDetector with OpenCV + optional TensorFlow CNN
- Image/video/camera detection pipeline
- GUI annotation tool (annotator.py)
- CNN model architecture, training script, batch prediction
- Dataset utilities for PKLot/CNRPark formats

Frontend (React 18):
- Protected routes with AuthContext (JWT)
- Dashboard with 30s polling and availability cards
- Interactive Leaflet map with lot markers and popups
- Login/Register forms with validation
- Admin dashboard with user table and lot management
- Responsive CSS with colour-coded availability

Tests:
- pytest fixtures: in-memory SQLite + httpx AsyncClient
- Auth endpoint tests (register, login, me, unauthorized)
- Parking endpoint tests (CRUD, role checks)
- Detector unit tests with mock CNN
- CNN model tests with mocked TensorFlow

Config:
- docker-compose.yml (postgres + backend + frontend)
- .env.example, pytest.ini, Dockerfiles, root requirements.txt
- Comprehensive docs: architecture, API, setup, user guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reduce ping worker log spam and fix duplicate thread start

- Slow ping from 1s to 10s (configurable via PING_INTERVAL env var)
  86,400 log lines/day → 8,640/day — same predictability data, way less noise
- Demote per-ping log from INFO to DEBUG so Render log ingestion isn't spammed
- Fix duplicate thread start: module-level start (works under Gunicorn/Render)
  was being shadowed by a dead __main__ block start that never ran on Render
- Error sleep increased from 5s to 30s on failure
- ENABLE_PING=0 now actually works on Render (was broken before)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Charts: Extract shared ChartLayout component for chart+legend layout (#47554)

* Charts: Add ChartLayout component for shared chart+legend layout

Introduces a ChartLayout component that encapsulates the shared
Stack + legend positioning pattern used by all chart components.
Includes comprehensive tests covering legend positioning, visibility
management, ref forwarding, and composition API slot rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Export ChartLayoutProps type from barrel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LineChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate BarChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate PieChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate PieSemiCircleChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LeaderboardChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Restore responsive/loading classes in LeaderboardChart empty state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: PieChart uses ChartLayout render prop for measurement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Add render prop and measurement support to ChartLayout

ChartLayout now accepts children as either ReactNode or a render prop
receiving ContentMeasurements (contentRef, contentWidth, contentHeight,
isMeasured). This allows charts to consolidate their useElementSize
calls into the shared layout component.

Adds waitForMeasurement prop that hides the layout until content is
measured. The legacy isWaitingForMeasurement prop is preserved as
deprecated for backward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Fix PieChart SingleChartContext.Provider placement

Move SingleChartContext.Provider to wrap ChartLayout (outside) so
composition API legends in legend slots can access the chart context.
Keep inner provider inside the render prop for measured dimensions.

Remove waitForMeasurement since PieChart uses fallback dimensions
(300x300) when unmeasured and doesn't need to hide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LineChart, BarChart, PieSemiCircleChart to ChartLayout render prop

Remove individual useElementSize calls from each chart and use
ChartLayout's render prop to receive content measurements instead.

Pattern: outer SingleChartContext.Provider with fallback dimensions
(for composition API legends in layout slots), inner provider inside
render prop with measured dimensions (for chart internals).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add changelog entries.

* test(charts): Simplify ChartLayout DOM order assertions

Replace the expectDomOrder helper with direct compareDocumentPosition
assertions. The helper required eslint-disable no-bitwise for the
masking check; inline expect(...).toBe(Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING)
is clearer and avoids the suppression.

Made-with: Cursor

* Charts: Extract shared CSS into ChartLayout, use Stack for pie centering

ChartLayout now wraps render prop children in a content div with shared
flex styles (flex: 1; min-height: 0; min-width: 0), removing duplicated
svg-wrapper rules from line-chart, bar-chart, pie-chart, and
pie-semi-circle-chart SCSS modules.

Charts no longer need to attach contentRef — ChartLayout handles
measurement internally via the content wrapper div.

Replace custom flex centering CSS in pie-chart and pie-semi-circle-chart
svg-wrappers with Stack components using align/justify props, per
project guidelines preferring layout primitives over ad-hoc flex rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant flex and inline styles from pie charts

The pie-chart class had `display: flex; flex-direction: column;
align-items: center` which duplicated what the Stack component
already provides. The `align-items: center` also prevented the
chart-layout content area from stretching to full width.

Similarly, the centering Stack inside both pie charts had inline
`style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` that is unnecessary
now that ChartLayout's content wrapper handles flex sizing.

Also removes hardcoded `size` props from two pie chart stories
that were masking the responsive sizing behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(charts): Remove superpowers plan from tracked files

Superpowers directories are now ignored via .git/info/exclude.
Removing the previously tracked plan file so it stays local.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Move ChartLayout to charts/private

ChartLayout is internal infrastructure consumed only by chart
components, not a public API. Moving it alongside other internal
modules (chart-composition, single-chart-context, with-responsive)
makes its scope clear and avoids exposing it in the components
directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Pass measured chart height to SingleChartContext in LineChart

SingleChartContext.chartHeight was set to the full container height
(including legend space), but AnnotationsOverlay uses this value to
size its SVG overlay. This caused misaligned annotations when a legend
was present because the overlay was sized larger than the actual chart
drawing area.

Lift the measured content height from the ChartLayout render prop into
state so the context provider reflects the true drawable area.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Add full dimensions to pie chart centering Stack

The centering Stack inside the ChartLayout render prop had no explicit
dimensions, so justify="center" had no effect because the Stack
collapsed to content height. Adding width/height 100% makes the Stack
fill the measured content area, enabling proper vertical centering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(charts): Fix misleading isMeasured docstring in ChartLayout

The docstring claimed isMeasured is "always true when
waitForMeasurement is not set", but isMeasured derives from
contentHeight > 0, which is false on initial render before
ResizeObserver fires regardless of waitForMeasurement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(charts): Add ChartLayout render-prop and waitForMeasurement tests

Cover the render-prop measurement behavior (function-as-children
receives contentWidth/contentHeight/isMeasured) and verify that
waitForMeasurement takes precedence over the legacy
isWaitingForMeasurement prop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove legacy isWaitingForMeasurement prop from ChartLayout

No callers pass isWaitingForMeasurement as a prop to ChartLayout —
line-chart and bar-chart only use it as a local variable name. Simplify
the visibility logic to only support waitForMeasurement and update
tests accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Use useEffect callback instead of setState during render

Calling setMeasuredChartHeight inside ChartLayout's render prop
triggered a React warning: "Cannot update a component while rendering
a different component". Add an onContentHeightChange callback prop to
ChartLayout that fires via useEffect after measurement, avoiding the
cross-component state update during render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(charts): Document waitForMeasurement render-prop requirement

waitForMeasurement only works with render-prop children because
contentRef is only attached in that code path. With plain ReactNode
children, isMeasured stays false and the layout remains permanently
hidden. Add JSDoc warning to prevent silent misuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove unused chartWidth/chartHeight from pie and leaderboard context

Pie, semi-circle, and leaderboard charts don't have consumers that
read chartWidth/chartHeight from SingleChartContext. Passing zeros was
misleading. Make the fields optional on ChartInstanceContextValue and
omit them from charts that don't provide meaningful values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Pass measured chart height to SingleChartContext in BarChart

Same pattern as LineChart: track the measured content height via
onContentHeightChange callback so SingleChartContext.chartHeight
reflects the actual drawable area, not the full container height.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Auto-hide ChartLayout during measurement for render-prop children

Replace the opt-in waitForMeasurement prop with automatic behavior:
when children is a render prop, ChartLayout applies visibility:hidden
until contentHeight > 0. This prevents layout shift consistently
across all charts without each chart needing to opt in.

Plain ReactNode children are unaffected since they don't depend on
measured dimensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Simplify measurement gating, mock element size in tests

ChartLayout now auto-hides render-prop children until measured, so
individual charts no longer need their own isWaitingForMeasurement
logic. Simplify bar/line charts to gate on chartHeight > 0 directly
and remove the now-unused hasLegend variables.

Add a getBoundingClientRect + ResizeObserver mock to the test setup so
useElementSize returns non-zero dimensions in JSDOM. Without this, the
auto-hide visibility guard keeps charts permanently hidden in tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant visx tooltip mock

The setup-visx-tooltip-mock.js mock overrode useTooltipInPortal to
provide non-zero containerBounds because JSDOM's getBoundingClientRect
returns zeros. Now that setup-element-size-mock.js globally mocks
getBoundingClientRect to return 800x400, the visx mock is redundant —
react-use-measure (used internally by useTooltipInPortal) gets non-zero
values directly from the source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style(charts): Move pie chart centering styles to CSS modules

Inline styles bypass the CSS module system and make overrides harder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Fix 6 test failures from element size mock

The setup-element-size-mock.js introduced in e242b7d had three flaws:

1. Accessed Element.prototype unconditionally, crashing the SSR test
   (jest-environment node) with "Element is not defined"

2. Returned hardcoded 800×400 for ALL zero-sized elements, overriding
   explicit dimensions (e.g. Sparkline at 100×40 got height 400)

3. The visx tooltip mock removed in 92201ce was NOT redundant —
   react-use-measure has a mounted.current guard that prevents state
   updates when ResizeObserver fires synchronously during the ref
   callback, leaving containerBounds at {0,0,...} and suppressing
   all tooltips

Fixes:
- Guard Element access with typeof check for SSR compatibility
- Walk up DOM tree for ancestor inline style dimensions instead of
  hardcoded fallback, so charts with explicit sizes measure correctly
- Restore visx tooltip mock with updated documentation explaining
  the react-use-measure timing issue
- Override getBoundingClientRect locally in chart-layout visibility
  test to simulate unmeasured state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Guard onContentHeightChange for render-prop mode

When children is a plain ReactNode, the content measurement ref is
never attached, so contentHeight stays 0. Firing
onContentHeightChange(0) in that case is misleading for callers who
wire up the callback without realizing it only produces meaningful
values in render-prop mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(charts): Wrap mock override in try/finally

Ensures the global getBoundingClientRect mock is restored even if
an assertion throws, preventing leak into subsequent tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Only call onContentHeightChange if measured

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(charts): Enhance early return condition in LineChartAnnotationsOverlay

Updated the early return condition to include checks for chartWidth and chartHeight, ensuring that the component only renders when all necessary dimensions are available. This prevents potential rendering issues when the chart is not properly sized.

* fix(charts): Pass args through in composition legend stories

Composition legend stories for bar, pie, donut, and semi-circle
were cherry-picking individual props instead of spreading args.
This meant Storybook controls like showLegend had no effect,
preventing dual legend rendering (showLegend + composition
<Chart.Legend>) that works in line chart and leaderboard stories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Changelog Bot <changelog-bot@automattic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace slide 6 single-line SVG with dual-curve lifecycle visual and add cited sources (#429)

* fix: replace slide 6 single-line SVG with dual-curve lifecycle visual and add cited sources

The individual slide page for Slide 6 (Technology Lifecycle Positioning) was
using an outdated single-line SVG that didn't match the dual-curve visual used
in slide-render.tsx and presentation-visuals.tsx. This replaces it with the
correct dual-curve chart showing Innovation Potential (cyan dashed) vs Adoption
Risk (amber solid) with the Leading Edge → Mainstream target zone.

Also enriches the lifecycle stage definitions in the markdown content with
real citations from Rogers (Diffusion of Innovations), Moore (Crossing the
Chasm), Gartner Hype Cycle, NIST SP 800-160, Microsoft Lifecycle Policy,
and CISA KEV catalog.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* chore: update package-lock.json after dependency install

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* feat: expand slide 6 with dual-curve analysis and real-world lifecycle examples

Add three substantive sections to slide 6 (Technology Lifecycle Positioning):

1. "Reading the Chart" — explains the dual-curve relationship between
   innovation potential and adoption risk, citing Christensen (Innovator's
   Dilemma), Gartner failure rates at Innovation Trigger, NIST Cybersecurity
   Framework 2.0 on aging infrastructure risk, and Rogers' 68% adoption
   window for early/late majority.

2. "Real-World Examples" — a 12-row table mapping concrete technologies
   (WebTransport, PQC, Rust, Deno/Bun, Node.js, React, PostgreSQL, jQuery,
   AngularJS, Windows 10, CentOS 7, Python 2.7) to their lifecycle stage
   with evidence from Stack Overflow 2024, DB-Engines, Gartner, NIST,
   Microsoft, Red Hat, CISA KEV, and others.

3. Expanded sources list (18 references) and three new speaker notes
   connecting the examples to the lifecycle model.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* feat: add lifecycle timeline charts for hardware, software, and supply chain examples

Add three new optional deep-dive slides (27-29) with proportional
timeline bar charts showing real technologies progressing through each
lifecycle phase. Each chart includes sourced phase durations that
explain why real adoption curves are asymmetric (imperfect S-curves):

- Slide 27: Hardware (HDDs) — 77-year lifecycle, right-skewed curve
  with 30-year mainstream plateau and rapid SSD displacement
- Slide 28: Software (Adobe Flash) — 25-year lifecycle, left-skewed
  with compressed 1-year EOL cliff after Apple/HTML5 displacement
- Slide 29: Supply Chain (Barcodes) — 83-year lifecycle, extremely
  long 22-year bleeding edge due to infrastructure dependencies

Includes dark-theme presentation visuals, light-theme landing page
versions, ASCII gallery entries, slide structure updates, and full
presentation markdown with cited sources (Rogers 2003, Computer
History Museum, GS1, IDC, Adobe, W3Techs, McKinsey).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: address PR #429 Copilot review feedback

- Replace underscore-based italics with asterisks for proper rendering
- Wrap bare URLs in markdown link syntax for clickability
- Remove role="img" from timeline bar; add comprehensive aria-label
- Add per-phase textColor for dark backgrounds (ef4444, 991b1b)
- Add missing slides 25-26 to teaching series data (closes gap)
- Extract shared LifecycleTimelineBar component to reduce duplication

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: address second round of PR #429 review feedback

- Fix remaining underscore italics missed in body text citations
- Align year-range labels to proportional widths matching phase bars
- Add role="group" with aria-roledescription to timeline bar
- Add slides 25-26 to optional-slide index in markdown
- Extract shared LIFECYCLE_CONFIGS data used by slide-render.tsx and
  presentation-visuals.tsx, eliminating phase data duplication
- Remove local TimelinePhase interface in favor of shared LifecyclePhase

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: format impact.json to pass CI prettier check

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: address PR #429 review feedback (round 3)

- Replace underscore italics with asterisk syntax in presentation deck
  markdown so the custom renderer (simple-markdown.tsx) correctly applies
  emphasis formatting
- Extract shared DualCurveChart component to eliminate code duplication
  between [slide]/page.tsx and slide-render.tsx
- Fix lifecycle-timeline-bar: add division-by-zero guard, use role="img"
  with aria-roledescription="chart" for accessibility, add title
  attributes on phase segments

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: support underscore emphasis in simple-markdown renderer

Prettier normalizes markdown emphasis to underscores (_text_), but the
custom simple-markdown renderer only matched asterisk emphasis (*text*).
Add underscore pattern as a fallback so italicized citations and labels
render correctly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: match both * and _ emphasis independently in simple-markdown

The previous ?? fallback meant underscore emphasis was only detected when
no asterisk match existed anywhere in the string. Now both patterns are
matched independently and the existing sort-by-index picks whichever
appears first, correctly handling mixed cases like `_a_ then *b*`.

Also exports tokenizeInline and adds 5 unit tests covering underscore
emphasis, asterisk emphasis, mixed usage, word-internal underscores,
and bold+emphasis combinations.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* fix: replace lookbehind regex with boundary-safe pattern for Safari

The negative lookbehind/lookahead in the underscore emphasis regex
(?<!\w)_..._(?!\w) throws a syntax error in older Safari/WebKit.
Replace with (^|[^\w])_..._([^\w]|$) and adjust the match object so
downstream tokenization code sees the same group layout.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

* refactor: remove fabricated RegExpMatchArray from underscore emphasis

Replace the fragile Object.assign match-object fabrication with a typed
InlineMatch interface that carries explicit value, consumed, label, and
href fields. This eliminates all match[0]/match[1] access patterns and
makes the underscore emphasis handling robust against future refactors.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01MfBfZjgvAG9NHm7HMgxaG4

- buildCurvePath now takes Array<[cp1, cp2, y1?, y2?]> instead of

- LifecycleTimelineChart derives a unique marker ID from the chart

feat(webchat): add token refresh, reconnect, card actions, analytics, escalation, branding, locale

- token-manager.ts: auto-refresh with configurable buffer, GET/POST fallback
- connection-manager.ts: exponential backoff (1s→30s), max 5 retries, idle timeout
- middleware/card-actions.ts: intercept Submit, ImBack, PostBack, OpenUrl actions

Tier 2 — Observability & Customization:
- analytics.ts: zero-dep event emitter, 9 event types, conversation metrics
- middleware/activity-logger.ts: debug logging for all activities
- middleware/typing-tracker.ts: typing duration + response latency measurement
- branding.ts: logo, header background, footer, bot name from env vars
- locale.ts: auto-detect browser locale, RTL support, VITE_LOCALE override

Tier 3 — Advanced Scenarios:
- escalation.ts: detect handoff.initiate/status/complete, manage escalation UI
- Security docs: token handling, CORS, CSP, rate limiting, iframe security
- Accessibility docs: WCAG 2.1 AA status, keyboard map, contrast ratios
- Markdown/HTML rendering docs: SDK rendering, sanitization, custom renderer

Wired all modules into main.ts middleware pipeline.
Comprehensive README rewrite: 13 sections replacing Microsoft Learn for this use case.

state including the typing indicator. Since IsBusy was already restored

discriminator.

[DeepTest] CUBIC Congestion Control Test Suite Enhancement (#5685)

## Summary

Rewrote `src/core/unittest/CubicTest.cpp` from 17 standalone tests (628
lines) to 50 fixture-based tests + 16 parametric `CubeRootTest` cases
(1754 lines).

## What changed

- **Test fixture** with shared helpers (`InitializeDefaultWithRtt`,
`EnterCongestionAvoidance`, `EnterHyStartActive`,
`MakeAckEvent`/`MakeLossEvent`) and named RTT constants for HyStart
thresholds.
- **16 parametric `CubeRootTest` cases** covering boundaries,
perfect/non-perfect cubes, and large inputs.
- **HyStart++ state transition table** in-file, mapping every transition
to its test.

## Test areas

| Area | Tests | What's covered |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Core CUBIC lifecycle | 14 | Initialization, reset, data
sent/invalidated/acked, loss, ECN, recovery entry/exit, fast
convergence, spurious congestion, zero-byte ACK, statistics, app-limited
API |
| Congestion avoidance & AIMD | 7 | CUBIC formula vs AIMD selection,
AIMD accumulator (half-rate, full-rate, overflow), sequential linear
convergence, Reno-friendly region, CUBIC overflow to BytesInFlightMax |
| Pacing | 3 | Slow start estimation (2×CW), congestion avoidance
estimation (1.25×CW), LastSendAllowance carryover |
| HyStart++ state machine | 16 | All 6 state transitions
(NOT_STARTED→ACTIVE, NOT_STARTED→DONE, ACTIVE→DONE×2,
ACTIVE→NOT_STARTED, DONE absorbing), delay increase/decrease detection,
Eta calculation, conservative round counting, disabled mode, state
persistence across recovery/spurious events, growth divisor invariant |
| Edge cases & boundaries | 8 | Minimum window floor, 2×BytesInFlightMax
clamp, bounded growth clamp (1.5×CW), time gap overflow, persistent
congestion window reset, double-loss guard, recovery stay-on-old-ACK,
ECN spurious rollback asymmetry |
| Send allowance & control | 2 | CanSend scenarios (7 states),
GetSendAllowance scenarios (5 states) |

## Coupling classification

| Category | Tests |
|----------|-------|
| Spec-conformance (RFC 8312, RFC 9002, HyStart++) | 23 |
| Implementation-specific, API-level assertions | 7 |
| Implementation-specific, internal struct assertions | 20 |

commit.message:
fix: import-paper-wallet when base-dir is absolute (#7720)

When wallet paths are already absolute (set during initialization via
`set_base_path`), creating a temp wallet for `import-paper-wallet`
silently reused the original wallet's database instead of an isolated
temp one.

## Root Cause

`WalletConfig::set_base_path` is a no-op for already-absolute paths:

```rust
pub fn set_base_path<P: AsRef<Path>>(&mut self, base_path: P) {
    if !self.data_dir.is_absolute() {  // skipped if already absolute
        self.data_dir = base_path.as_ref().join(self.data_dir.as_path());
    }
    // ...
}
```

So `new_config.set_base_path(temp_path.clone())` did nothing when
`base-dir` was absolute, causing the temp wallet to open the same DB as
the running wallet.

## Fix

Replace the `set_base_path` call with direct path assignments in the
`ImportPaperWallet` handler:

```rust
let mut new_config = config.clone();
// set_base_path is a no-op for already-absolute paths; assign directly
new_config.data_dir = temp_path.clone();
new_config.config_dir = temp_path.join("config");
new_config.db_file = temp_path.join("console_wallet.db");
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>import-wallet only works when base-dir is
relative.</issue_title>
> <issue_description>**Describe the bug**
> You can use the `import-paper-wallet` command to sweep funds from a
wallet into your current wallet.
> It does this by creating a temporary wallet using (almost) the same
Config as your original wallet.
> 
> I say _almost_, because the `data_dir` and `db_file` entries get
"absolutised" somewhere during wallet initialisation.
> 
> But the temp wallet creation process assumes that paths are relative,
based on
> 
> `new_config.set_base_path(temp_path.clone());`
> 
> and 
> 
> ```rust
>     pub fn set_base_path<P: AsRef<Path>>(&mut self, base_path: P) {
>         if !self.data_dir.is_absolute() {
> self.data_dir = base_path.as_ref().join(self.data_dir.as_path());
>         }
>         if !self.config_dir.is_absolute() {
> self.config_dir = base_path.as_ref().join(self.config_dir.as_path());
>         }
>         if !self.db_file.is_absolute() {
>             self.db_file = self.data_dir.join(self.db_file.as_path());
>         }
>         self.p2p.set_base_path(base_path);
>     }
> ```
> Dumping `WalletConfig` just before the call to `set_pase_path` and
running the wallet with
> 
> ```
> ./minotari_console_wallet -b ~/.tari import-paper-wallet --seed-words
'my seed phrase`
> 
> ...
> 
> 
> saving temp wallet in: "/home/user/.tari/nextnet/data/wallet/db/temp"
> 
> Shutting down wallet... Done.
> Invalid command. General error: The application exited. Cannot acquire
exclusive file lock, another instance of the application is already
running
> ```
> 
> ```
> WalletConfig { override_from: Some("nextnet"),
> <snip>
> data_dir: "/home/user/.tari/nextnet/data/wallet", config_dir:
"//home/user/.tari/nextnet/config/wallet", db_file:
"/home/user/.tari/nextnet/data/wallet/db/console_wallet.db",
> <snip>
> }
> ```
> 
> The absolute paths are set, even though the config is set to defaults:
> ```toml
> [wallet]
> # The relative path to store persistent data (default = "data/wallet")
> #data_dir = "data/wallet"
> #db_file = "db/console_wallet.db"
> 
> ```
> 
> If however, use run recovery with relative paths:

commit.message:
feat: `exclude_inputs` query parameter to `sync_utxos_by_block` endpoint (#7723)

The `sync_utxos_by_block` endpoint had no way to opt out of receiving
block inputs (spent UTXO hashes), forcing clients to receive data they
may not need.

## Changes

- **`SyncUtxosByBlockRequest` model** – added `exclude_inputs: bool`
(`#[serde(default)]`, defaults to `false`)
- **`SyncUtxosByBlockQueryParams`** – added matching `exclude_inputs`
query param with `#[param]` annotation; propagated through the `From`
impl and included in `Display` output
- **`fetch_utxos` in `query_service.rs`** – skips
`fetch_inputs_in_block` entirely when `exclude_inputs` is `true`, so no
inputs are fetched or returned
- **`http_api_guide.md`** – documents `exclude_inputs` (and the
pre-existing `exclude_spent`) in the query parameters table

## Usage

```
GET /sync_utxos_by_block?start_header_hash=<hash>&limit=5&page=0&exclude_inputs=true
```

When `exclude_inputs=true`, all `inputs` arrays in the response blocks
will be empty.

commit.message:
chore: remove unused wallet low power mode methods (#7719)

Low power mode methods in the wallet were unused and added dead code
throughout the stack. This removes them entirely.

### Removed
- `PowerMode` enum and `set_power_mode()` from `TransactionService`
- `SetLowPowerMode`/`SetNormalPowerMode` request variants and
`LowPowerModeSet`/`NormalPowerModeSet` response variants from the
transaction service API
- `set_low_power_mode()` / `set_normal_power_mode()` from
`TransactionServiceHandle`
- `wallet_set_low_power_mode` / `wallet_set_normal_power_mode` C FFI
functions and header declarations
- `low_power_polling_timeout` from `TransactionServiceConfig`, TOML
presets, and docs
- Dead `config` field from `TransactionService` struct (was only
referenced by the removed `set_power_mode`)

### Simplified
`timeout_update_watch` initialization now uses
`broadcast_monitoring_timeout` directly instead of branching on
`PowerMode::Normal` default:
```rust
// Before
let power_mode = PowerMode::default();
let timeout = match power_mode {
    PowerMode::Low => config.low_power_polling_timeout,
    PowerMode::Normal => config.broadcast_monitoring_timeout,
};
let timeout_update_watch = Watch::new(timeout);

// After
let timeout_update_watch = Watch::new(config.broadcast_monitoring_timeout);
```

feat: add OSC 7 CWD tracking for terminal sessions (#85)

Implement OSC 7 (Operating System Command 7) protocol support for
    real-time working directory tracking in terminal sessions. This is
    the standard protocol used by modern terminals (WezTerm, iTerm2,
    Ghostty) where the shell emits file:// URIs after each command.

    Changes:
- New osc7.rs parser module: extracts file:// URIs from raw PTY output
bytes, handling hostnames, percent-encoding, and both BEL and ESC\
terminators
- FilteringReader integration: scans passthrough bytes for OSC 7
sequences inline during PTY reads, sends CWD via mpsc channel
- Terminal consumes OSC 7 updates in sync(), updating pty_info.cwd so
existing consumers (tab duplication, etc.) get the new value
- Shell hook injection: sets PROMPT_COMMAND env var for bash, and
__KAZETERM_OSC7 for manual shell config integration
    - CWD priority: OSC 7 CWD takes precedence over sysinfo polling

    Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
